missions.title: Pull an Image
missions.story: Before you can deploy, you need the right image. Pull `redis:7-alpine` from Docker Hub — lightweight and battle-tested.
hints: `docker pull <image>:<tag>` downloads an image
`docker pull redis:7-alpine`
`docker pull redis:7-alpine`

missions.title: List and Remove Images
missions.story: The local image store is cluttered with old images consuming disk space. Remove the image `busybox:latest` to free space.
hints: `docker images` lists local images; `docker rmi <image>` removes one
`docker rmi busybox:latest`
`docker rmi busybox:latest`

missions.title: Your First Dockerfile
missions.story: It's time to build something. Create a Dockerfile that starts from `alpine:3.18`, sets maintainer label `maintainer=recruit`, and runs `echo "Mission Ready"` as the default command.
hints: A Dockerfile starts with `FROM`, then `LABEL`, then `CMD`
Write the Dockerfile above, then `docker build -t mission-image:v1 /workspace`

missions.title: ADD vs COPY
missions.story: Your app needs its source code inside the image. Use COPY to add `app.py` from the build context into `/app/app.py` in the image.
objectives: Write a Dockerfile using `FROM python:3.11-alpine`, `COPY app.py /app/app.py`, `CMD ["python", "/app/app.py"]`
hints: `COPY <src> <dest>` copies files from build context to image
Dockerfile with FROM, COPY, CMD; build with `docker build -t pyapp:v1 /workspace`
Write the Dockerfile, then `docker build -t pyapp:v1 /workspace`

missions.title: RUN Commands
missions.story: A bare OS image needs software installed. Build an image based on `ubuntu:22.04` that has `curl` installed.
objectives: Write a Dockerfile: `FROM ubuntu:22.04`, `RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl`
Verify: run a container from `curl-box:v1` and confirm `curl --version` works
hints: `RUN` executes commands during image build
Chain commands with `&&` to minimize layers
`RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl`

missions.title: ENV and WORKDIR
missions.story: Well-structured images define their working directory and bake in environment configuration. Build an image with `WORKDIR /app` and `ENV NODE_ENV=production`.
hints: `WORKDIR` sets the working directory; `ENV` sets environment variables
Both are Dockerfile instructions, one per line
Write the Dockerfile with all four instructions, then build

missions.title: Multi-Stage Build
missions.story: Bloated images are a security risk and waste bandwidth. Use a multi-stage build to compile a Go binary in one stage and copy only the binary into a minimal `scratch` or `alpine` final image.
objectives: Stage 1 (`builder`): `FROM golang:1.21-alpine AS builder`, copy `main.go`, run `go build -o app .`
Final image size should be under 20MB
hints: Multi-stage builds use multiple `FROM` lines; `COPY --from=<stage>` copies between stages
The builder stage has all dev tools; the final stage is lean
See the multi-stage Dockerfile structure in the objectives

initialState.summary: `/workspace/main.go` pre-created (a simple Hello World Go program)

missions.title: .dockerignore
missions.story: Your build context is huge because it includes `node_modules` and `.git`. Create a `.dockerignore` file to exclude them and speed up builds.
objectives: Build image `clean-build:v1` from the existing Dockerfile in `/workspace`
Verify the build context sent to daemon is under 1KB
hints: `.dockerignore` works like `.gitignore` — list patterns to exclude from build context
Add `node_modules` and `.git` on separate lines
Create `.dockerignore` with those two entries, then `docker build -t clean-build:v1 /workspace`

initialState.summary: `/workspace` contains a Dockerfile, a small `index.js`, large `node_modules/` (fake, 100MB), `.git/`
